We are in the Lower Broadway neighborhood just north of Downtown Newark which is home to a diverse population of Latinos and immigrants from other backgrounds. This means that great food, access to public transportation, shows and many other great perks are within walking distance.

This is a vibrant neighborhood that, like other key areas of Newark, is going through significant renewal and redevelopment, processes that are still very much under way (i.e., we are not as beautiful as we will soon be). If you don't let yourself be stopped by the couple of lots waiting redevelopment around us, you will find an array of small family-run restaurants and stores within 1-2 blocks radius of the apartment. host_about: Hi, my name is  Patrick Mamudu. I am co-hosting this humble abode with my mother, Catherine Luke.  We have lived in the Southward section of Newark, NJ since the early '80s.  Our current interests are traveling, shopping, teaching (in various public high schools in the city of Newark, New Jersey), cooking, attending church every Sunday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, STEM (science, technology, engineering, and math), movies, music, and medicine.  Our ethnic origin is Nigeria, and we take pride in our culture. We love wearing our native culture clothes and also cooking many of the staple dishes from there (jollof rice, fried rice, moi moi, akara, suya, pounded yam, etc). A fun fact about Catherine is that she has been to several European countries during the '90s in order to sell many of the native clothing that is a staple in our country. I have been to London, Italy, Belgium, Switzerland, Germany, Spain, France, Austria,  etc.  We are devout Christians and believe that God is the centerpiece of our life; he has been good to us.  Our faith allows us to be hospitable hosts who value all of our guests. We take the extra step in making sure your stay is comfortable and feels just like home. We are also helpful in regards to movement within the Greater New York region.

neighborhood_overview:
The Weequahic park neighborhood is one of Newark's oldest and most pleasant neighborhoods. The area features tree-lined streets, historic, large homes, and easy access to restaurants, shopping, and more. Weequahic park itself was designed by the Olmstead Brothers, the same designers who created Central Park in New York City.

We have owned this house since 2017. Since that time, we have never had any issues with crime or safety, but we encourage you to research the neighborhood online to ensure you will be comfortable and to calibrate your expectations.

neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark is a historic district consisting of early 20th-century towers. It contains the central business district and many art deco warehouses and offices that have been converted into residences. Newark offers a wide array of things to do.  There are parks, local and national restaurants, shopping, Museums, the Arts, Sports and a variety of other activities.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

Accommodations at the Kislak Building present a modern take on Newark’s historic character, combining contemporary finishes with inviting interiors, providing all the comforts of home to make it a perfect place to stay for business travelers, professionals, extended stay guests, families, or tourists visiting Newark, New York City or the surrounding areas.




These newly renovated one, two, and three-bedroom apartments are an alternative to cramped downtown hotels. Check-in and check-out are
neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark is a historic district consisting of early 20th-century towers. It contains the central business district and many art deco warehouses and offices that have been converted into residences. Newark offers a wide array of things to do.  There are parks, local and national restaurants, shopping, Museums, the Arts, Sports and a variety of other activities.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

name: Meditation & Tranquility Rm Newark 7 mins to Penn
description:
Welcome to your peaceful and quiet stay. The bedroom has a double bed, both neat and spacious with a neat and clean bathroom. Wind down with luxury sheets and high end amenities.

A 7 min drive or train ride to Penn Station and 14 min drive to EWR.

Area transportation is convenient. We are located 2 blocks from the Park Ave Train Station; 1 block from Dunkin Donuts, KFC, Dominican restaurant, laundromat, cleaners, and 2 delis; and 5 min walk to grocery.
neighborhood_overview:
Newark is the Garden State’s largest metropolis and is located just 8 short miles from Manhattan. Air, water, roadway, railway, bike path, footbridge and subway, Newark offers every manner of transportation imaginable, providing access that’s unbelievable.

The transportation in the Roseville neighborhood is super convenient! After taking the light rail to Newark Penn Station 2 blocks away, its only a 20 minute commute to Midtown, NYC by train.

For entertainment, the New Jersey Performing Arts Center (NJPAC). Take on the largest premium outlet mall in the state, The Outlet Collection, Jersey Gardens, welcoming over 18 million visitors a year. Other activities include the Newark Museum; NHL New Jersey Devils; and NCAA Seton Hall Men’s Basketball that you can watch close by at the Prudential Center.

If you visit in the spring, experience the largest collection of cherry blossoms in the nation. Branch Brook Park blossoms with over 4,300 cherry trees, and h
host_about: You will find these Airbnbs to be bright, airy, and peaceful places for contemplation, studying, reading, working, and creating positive energy. There are lots of public transportation close by (trains and buses) as well as places to grab a bite. Don't hesitate to reach out: we look forward to hearing from you and hosting you!

neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark is a historic district consisting of early 20th-century towers. It contains the central business district and many art deco warehouses and offices that have been converted into residences. Newark offers a wide array of things to do.  There are parks, local and national restaurants, shopping, Museums, the Arts, Sports and a variety of other activities.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

neighborhood_overview: Newark Ironbound is a culinary and cultural gem. neighborhood_overview:
The suite is located in the Weequahic neighborhood of Newark, NJ.

•》Just a short drive from Newark Penn Station, Prudential Center, NJ Performing Arts Center, MetLife Stadium, Nickolodean at American Dream Mall, art and museums downtown.

•》Located less than two blocks from Weequahic Park, home to 311 acres of lush green space.

• 》Weequahic Park was designed by Olmstead, known for designing some of the world’s most famous parks that includes NYC’s Central Park and Brooklyn’s Prospect Park.

•》The park has the world’s longest resilient-surfaced (100% recycled rubber) track which wraps around a beautiful 80 acre lake, with fountain and 18 hole golf course.

•》Newark, NJ is rich in history, home to the state’s largest museum and the largest port on the east coast.
host_about:
myCitySuite is a boutique short-term rental company offering both vacation rentals and event spaces.

About the Founder:

Defina helps to sculpt the NYC skyline for a living. Essentially, she puts up the steel structure of buildings & bridges. This has created in her, a fixation on city skylines across the world!

From cross country road trips to snorkeling in Cuba, her love for discovering new places & new experiences keeps life exciting.

She enjoys meeting new people, music, interior design, education, & fashion. When she is not putting up a new building or repairing one of NY's iconic bridges, she is out exploring her interests or indulging in some quality time with her daughters.

From Defina and the myCitySuite team, we welcome you to create memories with purpose and experience travel with style!

neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark is a historic district consisting of early 20th-century towers. It contains the central business district and many art deco warehouses and offices that have been converted into residences. Newark offers a wide array of things to do.  There are parks, local and national restaurants, shopping, Museums, the Arts, Sports and a variety of other activities.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark is a historic district consisting of early 20th-century towers. It contains the central business district and many art deco warehouses and offices that have been converted into residences. Newark offers a wide array of things to do.  There are parks, local and national restaurants, shopping, Museums, the Arts, Sports and a variety of other activities.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

IN NEWARK:

One of the premier cultural institutions in Newark is the Newark Museum of Art. It stands as one of the most comprehensive museums in the state, including a large collection of some 30,000 pieces of world-class Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Southeast Asian, Oceanic, and Tibetan art. The true star of the show is the Tibetan Buddhist altar – consecrated in 1990 by His Holiness the Fourteenth Dalai Lama himself. In addition, the museum boasts a planetarium, where you can explore the myths and legends of one of the most visible constellations in our night sky.

Another must-see destination in Newark is the Prudential Center. This state-of-the-art arena is home to the New Jersey Devils professional hockey team and also hosts a wide array of concerts and events – with big names such as John Mayer, Carrie Underwood, Jeff Dunham and many more!

neighborhood_overview:
University Heights is a vibrant and diverse neighborhood located in the heart of Newark, New Jersey. As the name suggests, it is home to several major universities, including Rutgers University, New Jersey Institute of Technology, and Essex County College. The neighborhood has a lively atmosphere with many young students and professionals.

In addition to the universities, University Heights is also home to several museums, such as the Newark Museum, the New Jersey Historical Society, and the Institute of Jazz Studies. The neighborhood is also known for its beautiful parks, including Branch Brook Park, which boasts the largest collection of cherry blossom trees in the United States.

University Heights is a foodie's paradise, with a wide variety of restaurants serving cuisines from all over the world. From Mexican street food to Italian delicacies, there is something for everyone.

neighborhood_overview:
University Heights is a vibrant and diverse neighborhood located in the heart of Newark, New Jersey. As the name suggests, it is home to several major universities, including Rutgers University, New Jersey Institute of Technology, and Essex County College. The neighborhood has a lively atmosphere with many young students and professionals.

In addition to the universities, University Heights is also home to several museums, such as the Newark Museum, the New Jersey Historical Society, and the Institute of Jazz Studies. The neighborhood is also known for its beautiful parks, including Branch Brook Park, which boasts the largest collection of cherry blossom trees in the United States.

University Heights is a foodie's paradise, with a wide variety of restaurants serving cuisines from all over the world. From Mexican street food to Italian delicacies, there is something for everyone.

neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark is a historic district consisting of early 20th-century towers. It contains the central business district and many art deco warehouses and offices that have been converted into residences. Newark offers a wide array of things to do.  There are parks, local and national restaurants, shopping, Museums, the Arts, Sports and a variety of other activities.

The Prudential Center, located in the heart of downtown, is home to the New Jersey Devils, other local sports teams and hosts many national concert tours. Also located in Downtown Newark are the New Jersey Museum of Art and the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

From Craft Breweries to specialty local fare, Downtown Newark will not disappoint your hunger and thirst.

There are many events that take place on a year round basis that attracts locals and tourists alike.

If you run out of things to do in Newark, New York City is a 20 minute ride train ride away.

neighborhood_overview:
Your Airbnb is located in Weequahic (pronounced Wee-QUAY-ic, or WEEK-wake "when spoken rapidly." Weequahic is a neighborhood in the city of Newark in Essex County, in the U.S. state of New Jersey. Part of the South Ward, it is separated from Clinton Hill by Hawthorne Avenue on the north, and bordered by the township of Irvington on the west, Newark Liberty International Airport and Dayton on the east, and Hillside Township and the city of Elizabeth on the south.

Part of the Weequahic neighborhood has been designated a historic district; major streets are Lyons Avenue, Bergen Street, and Chancellor Avenue.[2][3] Newark Beth Israel Medical Center is a major long-time institution in the neighborhood.

neighborhood_overview: Newark Ironbound is a culinary and cultural gem. Called the “Ironbound" after the large metalworking industry in the area from the early nineteenth century, the area has a distinct portugese and Brazilian culture from the wave of immigration from portugal in the 20th century and brazil in the past few decades. Some of the best barbecue and seafood restaurants can be found within walking distance from this apartment. My personal favorites are Sihana cafe and Mompou for casual fare - breakfast or lunch and fornos, sol de mar & Seabra’s marisquera for nice elaborate dinners.

name: Women Hosted Nature Nirvana | Near NYC + Airport
description: Welcome to The Clifton B+B | A Botanical Bed + Breakfast, a peaceful women-owned retreat with spa vibes and greenery near NYC/Airport. Enjoy FREE breakfast, welcome snacks, lush plants, a soaking tub, and soft natural light. NO cleaning fee. Fast WiFi + desk space support remote work. Located in Newark’s Forest Hill Historic District, steps from Branch Brook Park, this sanctuary is designed with women solo travelers, professionals, creatives, and students  in mind seeking comfort and stillness.
neighborhood_overview:
Welcome to the Forest Hill Historic District, a serene oasis in bustling Newark! Discover a neighborhood where city living meets natural charm. Take leisurely strolls along tree-lined streets, marveling at the eclectic mix of homes blending classic elegance with modern flair.

Immerse yourself in nature's embrace with our lush parks and green spaces, perfect for picnics and relaxation amidst the soothing symphony of birds singing. The neighborhood is bounded on the west by Branch Brook Park. The park is noted for the largest collection of cherry blossom trees in the United States, having over 5,000 in more than eighteen different varieties collectively called Cherry Blossom Land, as well as its spectacular Cherry Blossom Festival each April. Perfect for a bike or scooter ride. There are also tennis courts in our beautiful park and an indoor skating rink!

Experience the warmth of community as friendly neighbors gather for "Porchfest", yard sales, house tours, ca
host_about: I am a Garden + Plant Creative who lives in a jungle of lush plants at The Clifton B+B | A Botanical Bed and Breakfast, listed here.  I savor Asian + Latin food.  Love nature, art, learning Spanish, meeting creative folks and tracing my roots through genealogy.  Plant design, writing, travel, gardening and photography fuel my creativity, while House Music, Amapiano, Jazz, Salsa and Bossa Nova set the vibe.  Wanderlust is my middle name.

Ironbound District:
The Ironbound, also known as "Little Portugal," is one of Newark's most vibrant and diverse neighborhoods. Famous for its rich cultural heritage, the area boasts a bustling atmosphere filled with the flavors, sounds, and traditions of Portuguese, Brazilian, and Spanish communities. You'll find an array of authentic restaurants, cafes, and bakeries that serve mouthwatering pastries, tapas, and seafood dishes.

Dining and Entertainment:
Providence Street is just a short walk from Ferry Street, the main artery of the Ironbound, where you can indulge in some of the best dining experiences Newark has to offer. From cozy cafes and family-owned eateries to trendy bars and upscale restaurants, there’s something to satisfy every palate. Don't miss out on the local favorites like Portuguese BBQ, fresh seafood, and delicious pastries.

neighborhood_overview:
Ironbound District:
The Ironbound, also known as "Little Portugal," is one of Newark's most vibrant and diverse neighborhoods. Famous for its rich cultural heritage, the area boasts a bustling atmosphere filled with the flavors, sounds, and traditions of Portuguese, Brazilian, and Spanish communities. You'll find an array of authentic restaurants, cafes, and bakeries that serve mouthwatering pastries, tapas, and seafood dishes.

Dining and Entertainment:
Providence Street is just a short walk from Ferry Street, the main artery of the Ironbound, where you can indulge in some of the best dining experiences Newark has to offer. From cozy cafes and family-owned eateries to trendy bars and upscale restaurants, there’s something to satisfy every palate. Don't miss out on the local favorites like Portuguese BBQ, fresh seafood, and delicious pastries.

host_about: Hi, my name is  Patrick Mamudu. I am co-hosting this humble abode with my mother, Catherine Luke.  We have lived in the Southward section of Newark, NJ since the early '80s.  Our current interests are traveling, shopping, teaching (in various public high schools in the city of Newark, New Jersey), cooking, attending church every Sunday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, STEM (science, technology, engineering, and math), movies, music, and medicine.  Our ethnic origin is Nigeria, and we take pride in our culture. We love wearing our native culture clothes and also cooking many of the staple dishes from there (jollof rice, fried rice, moi moi, akara, suya, pounded yam, etc). A fun fact about Catherine is that she has been to several European countries during the '90s in order to sell many of the native clothing that is a staple in our country. I have been to London, Italy, Belgium, Switzerland, Germany, Spain, France, Austria,  etc.  We are devout Christians and believe that God is the centerpiece of our life; he has been good to us.  Our faith allows us to be hospitable hosts who value all of our guests. We take the extra step in making sure your stay is comfortable and feels just like home. We are also helpful in regards to movement within the Greater New York region.

neighborhood_overview:
Downtown Newark has a variety of activities and attractions to offer:

1. Newark Museum of Art: This museum features a diverse collection of American art, decorative arts, contemporary art, and works from Asia, Africa, and the Americas.

2. New Jersey Performing Arts Center (NJPAC): Enjoy a wide range of performances, including concerts, theater, dance, and more.

3. Ironbound District: Known for its vibrant Portuguese and Brazilian communities, this area is great for exploring restaurants, cafes, and shops.

4. Military Park: A historic park with beautiful green spaces, public art, and regular events.

name: All-Brick Home Minutes From EWR Room #3
description: Forget your worries in this spacious and serene space. Located less than 10 minutes from Newark International Airport, this space is a bedroom equipped with a spacious closet, a bathroom right next door in the apartment, and a comfortable queen size bed. Ample sunlight can come in from the window with adjustable blinds that also promote privacy. Great for couples. Access to living room with 43 inch tv and large kitchen.
host_about: Hi, my name is  Patrick Mamudu. I am co-hosting this humble abode with my mother, Catherine Luke.  We have lived in the Southward section of Newark, NJ since the early '80s.  Our current interests are traveling, shopping, teaching (in various public high schools in the city of Newark, New Jersey), cooking, attending church every Sunday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, STEM (science, technology, engineering, and math), movies, music, and medicine.  Our ethnic origin is Nigeria, and we take pride in our culture. We love wearing our native culture clothes and also cooking many of the staple dishes from there (jollof rice, fried rice, moi moi, akara, suya, pounded yam, etc). A fun fact about Catherine is that she has been to several European countries during the '90s in order to sell many of the native clothing that is a staple in our country. I have been to London, Italy, Belgium, Switzerland, Germany, Spain, France, Austria,  etc.  We are devout Christians and believe that God is the centerpiece of our life; he has been good to us.  Our faith allows us to be hospitable hosts who value all of our guests. We take the extra step in making sure your stay is comfortable and feels just like home. We are also helpful in regards to movement within the Greater New York region.

neighborhood_overview:
Branch Brook Park (15-minute walk):

Branch Brook Park is stunning area of natural beauty that spans over 360 acres and boasts the largest collection of Japanese cherry blossom trees in the United States, with over 5,300 trees in 18 varieties.

Designed by the Olmsted Brothers, the park features a blend of landscapes, including the formal Prudential Concert Grove, the beautifully landscaped Ballantine Gardens, and the peaceful Helen and William M. Lyons Lake.

Its winding paths, such as the Park Avenue Promenade and the Branch Brook Park Drive, offer scenic walks and bike rides of the park’s bridges, lakes, and wooded areas.

Additionally, the park is a playground of possibilities with its diverse recreational facilities, including tennis courts where you can perfect your serve, a lively roller-skating rink for some retro fun, and playgrounds that spark joy for children of all ages.

Come April, the park transforms into a vibrant fest

host_about: Hi, my name is  Patrick Mamudu. I am co-hosting this humble abode with my mother, Catherine Luke.  We have lived in the Southward section of Newark, NJ since the early '80s.  Our current interests are traveling, shopping, teaching (in various public high schools in the city of Newark, New Jersey), cooking, attending church every Sunday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, STEM (science, technology, engineering, and math), movies, music, and medicine.  Our ethnic origin is Nigeria, and we take pride in our culture. We love wearing our native culture clothes and also cooking many of the staple dishes from there (jollof rice, fried rice, moi moi, akara, suya, pounded yam, etc). A fun fact about Catherine is that she has been to several European countries during the '90s in order to sell many of the native clothing that is a staple in our country. I have been to London, Italy, Belgium, Switzerland, Germany, Spain, France, Austria,  etc.  We are devout Christians and believe that God is the centerpiece of our life; he has been good to us.  Our faith allows us to be hospitable hosts who value all of our guests. We take the extra step in making sure your stay is comfortable and feels just like home. We are also helpful in regards to movement within the Greater New York region.

name: Colonial Home Next to EWR and NYC! Best Deal #2
description: A colonial home that has maple wooden trim and tan colors on the inside. A fireplace is also available that we do not use and that isn't active but makes for nice decoration. All brick exterior with tile roofing and white pillars on the front porch. Lots of leather sofas and maple wooden trimmed dining table is also available. Conveniently located next to Newark International Airport and Downtown Newark (5 mins away, details in additional information), as well as parks and stores.
host_about: Hi, my name is  Patrick Mamudu. I am co-hosting this humble abode with my mother, Catherine Luke.  We have lived in the Southward section of Newark, NJ since the early '80s.  Our current interests are traveling, shopping, teaching (in various public high schools in the city of Newark, New Jersey), cooking, attending church every Sunday, Tuesday, and Wednesday, STEM (science, technology, engineering, and math), movies, music, and medicine.  Our ethnic origin is Nigeria, and we take pride in our culture. We love wearing our native culture clothes and also cooking many of the staple dishes from there (jollof rice, fried rice, moi moi, akara, suya, pounded yam, etc). A fun fact about Catherine is that she has been to several European countries during the '90s in order to sell many of the native clothing that is a staple in our country. I have been to London, Italy, Belgium, Switzerland, Germany, Spain, France, Austria,  etc.  We are devout Christians and believe that God is the centerpiece of our life; he has been good to us.  Our faith allows us to be hospitable hosts who value all of our guests. We take the extra step in making sure your stay is comfortable and feels just like home. We are also helpful in regards to movement within the Greater New York region.

neighborhood_overview: Newark Ironbound is a culinary and cultural gem. Called the “Ironbound" after the large metalworking industry in the area from the early nineteenth century, the area has a distinct portugese and Brazilian culture from the wave of immigration from portugal in the 20th century and brazil in the past few decades. Some of the best barbecue and seafood restaurants can be found within walking distance from this apartment. My personal favorites are Sihana cafe and Mompou for casual fare - breakfast or lunch and fornos, sol de mar & Seabra’s marisquera for nice elaborate dinners.

Welcome to the Ironbound – Newark’s Hidden Gem 🇵🇹🍷🎶
Nestled just minutes from Newark Penn Station, the Ironbound is one of the city’s most vibrant and culturally rich neighborhoods. Known for its Portuguese, Spanish, and Brazilian roots, this area is a food lover’s dream, offering world-class steakhouses, bakeries, tapas bars, and authentic churrascarias all within walking distance.
🛍️ What to Explore:
Walk along Ferry Street, the heart of Ironbound, lined with local shops, cozy cafés, and lively restaurants. Stop by Independence Park for some fresh air or a casual stroll.
🍷 Where to Eat & Drink:
Enjoy an espresso at Teixeira’s Bakery, dinner at the legendary Forno’s of Spain, or drinks and live music at a local fado bar. Weekend nightlife is spirited but never overwhelming.

4. Fairmount: Located to the north, Fairmount is a residential area with local shops and schools.

neighborhood_overview: Newark Ironbound is a culinary and cultural gem. Called the “Ironbound" after the large metalworking industry in the area from the early nineteenth century, the area has a distinct portugese and Brazilian culture from the wave of immigration from portugal in the 20th century and brazil in the past few decades. Some of the best barbecue and seafood restaurants can be found within walking distance from this apartment. My personal favorites are Sihana cafe and Mompou for casual fare - breakfast or lunch and fornos, sol de mar & Seabra’s marisquera for nice elaborate dinners.
